## [00:00 - 03:01]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about what are the best offers to sell on webinars. So you should know that you can create a webinar to help sell almost anything. I've seen webinars work in selling courses and software and what we call SAS or software as a service. That's the subscription things where you sign up for something and pay every month for it for selling real estate. I've seen it work for selling individuals, selling their own house without having to use a real estate agent. I've seen it working for coaching and consulting. I've seen beauticians use it successfully. Lawyers. I've seen companies use it to recruit people to work in their companies. I've seen people sell books using webinars. Of course, e-commerce products, selling companies. Hey, I've even seen people use it to get dates. I've seen it used to sell membership sites, travel destinations, using very effectively to fill live conferences, to sell a mastermind program, a high-end, 20, 30, 50 thousand dollar mastermind. I've even seen colleges use it to increase their enrollment and much, much more. Basically, you can sell just about anything using a webinar, but there are certain things that tend to work better on webinars than others. And if you're trying, when you are trying to decide what you're going to sell on your webinar, there's one key question to consider in this process, which is, will you be using paid advertising to get people to attend? So if you've got a big list of people that are likely buyers for your product and you don't need to run paid advertising, hey, you could, in that condition, you can sell almost anything and have it be profitable. But if you are going to use paid advertising, there are certain offers that are more likely to be profitable than others. And when it comes right down to it, there are eight common characteristics of offers that work on webinars. So the ideal price point of a product to be sold on a webinar is $497 to $2,500. Now, I've seen people make $197 or $97 work. In fact, I've run many webinars using those price points, but they need to have an upsell in place behind that, something that brings in additional dollars other than just the base $97 or $197 product price in order to be able to profitably run a webinar using paid ads. If you're getting higher than $2,500, you can make a webinar, definitely work, but you're probably not going to be able to just say, click here to buy. You're probably going to have to have a one-on-one enrollment conversation after the webinar

## [03:01 - 06:03]

to get them to commit to buying. So the webinar form, webinar is actually selling them on filling out an application to be able to have that one-on-one conversation. Now, low price products can definitely work on webinars, as long as you already have a list of potential buyers or some kind of free traffic source. It's tough to make them work if you have to buy traffic using ads or even affiliates. Now, note I put affiliates in this. You can get affiliate traffic to a low-dollar offer and make it work. The problem is that most affiliates want to have a higher-dollar offer so that they get a higher amount of pay for the people that they send to you. And so there are many affiliates who just aren't willing to market a webinar for someone else unless the price point is high enough that it makes it worth their time and effort to do so. Second criteria, there's a real unmet need. So people's lives are being negatively affected by the problem that your webinar is going to solve and they're struggling to try to solve it. And ideally, there are strong negative emotions attached to the problem or their inability to solve it. So if people just aren't that concerned about that problem, even if the problem is real, it probably won't work as well as if you're solving a problem that they really, it really bothers them that this problem is not solved. So real unmet need. Next, it's a need that people are willing to pay money to solve that particular problem. So the problem is important enough to them that they're willing to pay a significant amount of money to solve that problem in their life. So there are many people who have a problem that they're just not willing to pay money to get to solve that particular problem. So those situations are tough to be able to sell on a webinar because you can't get their emotions to a high enough point to be willing to pay because they're saying, Hey, I've had this problem for 20 years, it's never really bothered me enough to spend any money on it. Why? Why would I want to spend a lot of money to solve that problem now? Next, it's a problem that people are able to pay money to solve. So there are some real needs, but the people who have them aren't physically able to pay enough money so that you can make money to get those problems solved. A great example of this is getting

## [06:03 - 09:07]

approved for Social Security disability in the United States. So Social Security disability pays you a certain amount for the rest of your life without you having to work. There are a lot of people who have an accident who get emotional or mental problems or whatever, and they would like to be able to get Social Security disability, they can't hold down a job, but they just don't have enough money to be able to pay you $500 to sell them something to help them do that. And so one of the criteria is you want to make sure that what you're going to sell actually has an audience who's able to pay money to get that problem solved. Next criteria, you can actually provide proof that your solution works, whether that's in the form of case studies or testimonials or real results that you can show them I did this and here was my bank account before, here's my bank account afterwards, or here's my income graph or whatever that is. Number six, the product sparks emotion. So your target audience, if you really want to succeed, needs to be passionate about the solution the product provides. Now I showed this particular slide to someone who is helping me with this program and she said, so what kind of product doesn't have emotions? And I said air fryers. And she immediately went, oh, okay, I get it. Now I realize that there are a certain group of people to whom air fryers are a strongly emotional element in their life. But unless you're able to attract that group of people who that air fryer is strongly emotional, you're probably you're possibly not going to do that well with that particular webinar. So it helps if the product that you're selling actually has an emotional context to it. And speaking about emotions, you're passionate about the product and the transformation that it provides, that passion or lack of it will come through in your tone of voice and the energy you put into your webinar. And you need to have passion to convey the dream of the better life to others in your webinars, which is one of the key things that we are communicating in a webinar is, hey, here's this problem, your life can be better. Here's how you make it better by my product. And number eight, the product is differentiated in the marketplace. So ideally, you're able to tell people why the solution you provide is better than that that they can receive elsewhere. I'm not sure I would want to try to market sugar on a webinar, why is my sugar better than the sugar that you can buy at the grocery store. And so that's a commodity product. If you've got something where

## [09:07 - 10:33]

you're no different than everyone else, I'd be a little hesitant to try to sell that on a webinar. And whatever that reason is that says that that you're different, that reason needs to be convincing so that they actually believe it. So an ideal product for a webinar is one where you can show them the problems that their lack of solution creates in their lives. You can show them the emotional impact of that problem and can paint a picture of their life with the problem solved that they can then aspire to achieve. And that's all elements that you're going to do within the webinar. Now there are certain products that don't perform as well in webinars, especially webinars where you are needing to run paid traffic into that webinar. So the first one is products that don't meet the eight criteria listed above. And secondly is low dollar products. I realized that was one of them, but I really want to stress unless you've got a list of potential buyers and a strong back end, I'd prefer you to choose a product that was in the $497 to $2,500 product price range because you'll just tend to do better with the webinar selling a product in that price range.
